pi_items.source: IHE PCD DEC profile documentation (wiki.ihe.net/index.php/PCD_Profile_DEC_Overview) explicitly lists 'vital signs monitors' as example patient care devices used in DEC profile; Multiparameter Monitor is standard terminology for multi-parameter vital signs monitoring devices

FDA regulatory documentation and multiple manufacturers (Analog Devices, ADInstruments, BIOPAC, Nihon Kohden) confirm NIBP as standard clinical abbreviation for Non-Invasive Blood Pressure measurement devices; FDA classifies NIBP monitors under 21 CFR §870.1130

IHE PCD Profile DEC Overview documentation confirms Device Observation Reporter (DOR) as the actor that 'receives data from PCDs, including those based on proprietary formats, and maps the received data to transactions providing consistent syntax and semantics'

IHE PCD Profile DEC Overview documentation confirms Device Observation Consumer (DOC) as 'the actor responsible for receiving PCD data from the Device Observation Reporter, the Device Observation Filter, or both'

OpenICE documentation states 'OMG DDS standard middleware' is core to OpenICE platform; OpenICE project incorporates RTI Connext DDS runtime for publish-subscribe medical device communication; DDS Foundation confirms DDS use in Medical Device Plug-and-Play (MD PnP) program reference implementation

Task configuration parameter in task.json metadata field; used as pass/fail threshold in verifier.py line 134-135 with configurable scoring (maximum 100 points based on device detection, screenshots, and report quality)

Task specification in task.json description explicitly requires 'Write a formal IHE DEC conformance test report to /home/ga/Desktop/dec_conformance_report.txt'; export_result.sh validates this file location
Task specification in task.json description requires 'Save a screenshot showing the Vital Signs app with device data to: /home/ga/Desktop/conformance_evidence_vitals.png'
Task specification in task.json description requires 'Save a screenshot showing the OpenICE Supervisor device list to: /home/ga/Desktop/conformance_evidence_devices.png'

privileged_info_summary: The task is a simulated IHE (Integrating the Healthcare Enterprise) DEC (Device Enterprise Communication) conformance pre-test using OpenICE Supervisor. The metadata correctly identifies real clinical standards and technologies: DOR (Device Observation Reporter) and DOC (Device Observation Consumer) are standardized actors defined in the IHE PCD DEC profile; Multiparameter Monitor and NIBP (Non-Invasive Blood Pressure) are verified medical device types used in clinical monitoring; DDS (Data Distribution Service) is the confirmed middleware standard implemented in OpenICE for publish-subscribe device communication. The task requires agents to create two simulated device adapters, launch a clinical application (Vital Signs), capture evidence screenshots, and write a conformance report with specific sections (TEST IDENTIFICATION, DEVICE UNDER TEST, CLINICAL APPLICATION, DATA FLOW VERIFICATION, and OVERALL CONFORMANCE ASSESSMENT). All expected file paths and content requirements are correctly specified.
